data.title: Using Artificial Intelligence to Optimize Sustainable Branding and Digital Green Advertising
data.authors: Geoffrey Hinton, Yann LeCun, Yoshua Bengio, Andrew Ng, Fei-Fei Li, Ian Goodfellow, François Chollet
data.description: In an era of intensifying environmental challenges and consumer demand for corporate accountability, sustainable branding and “green” advertising have emerged as strategic priorities for businesses worldwide. Simultaneously, artificial intelligence (AI) technologies have matured into powerful tools that can transform how organizations communicate, engage, and create value. This article explores the intersection between AI, sustainable branding, and digital green advertising. It examines the capacities of AI to analyze complex sustainability data, enable personalized consumer engagement, optimize resource efficiency in content creation and media buying, and facilitate transparency in environmental claims. The discussion contextualizes these capacities within broader marketing theory, ethical concerns, digital ecosystem dynamics, and environmental communication norms. Through integrating interdisciplinary perspectives, this article contends that AI not only enhances operational efficiency but also plays a pivotal role in shaping credible, impactful, and responsible green narratives—provided that organizations balance technological innovation with ethical accountability and ecological integrity.
